message.content:
Tool-calling agents need to avoid repeated tool loops, where the agent ends up calling the same tool repeatedly in a loop. This can be prevented by setting an upper limit on the number of tool calls the agent can make. Each time the agent calls a tool, a counter is incremented. When the counter reaches the upper limit, the agent stops making tool calls and proceeds with the next steps.

Additionally, the agent should be designed to recognize patterns of behavior that lead to repeated tool loops. If the agent detects that it is calling the same tool repeatedly, it can stop the loop and either move on to the next step or try a different tool.

Finally, the agent should be designed to learn from its mistakes. If the agent detects that it is stuck in a loop, it can adjust its behavior to avoid the loop in the future. This can involve changing the order in which the agent calls tools, or changing the criteria the agent uses to decide which tool to call.
